revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kamikikikatsu]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨津臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = [[建稲穂命]]、[[建御世狭名命]]、[[兄勝命]]、[[弟勝命]]？ | 妻 = [[美加々比売命]] | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]]、[[伊己呂比命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 『[[常陸国風土記]]』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に登場する人物で、同書によると[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に神が[[二上山|大坂山]]頂に白妙の大御服を着て顕現し、白鉾の御杖を取って神勅を下したとされる。そこで天皇は八十の伴緒を召集し、神託を下した神について問うと、大中臣神聞勝命が「大八嶋国は汝が知ろすべき国と言向け賜った、香嶋国に坐す[[タケミカヅチ|天津大御神]]（鹿島神）の下された教戒です」と答えた。天皇はこの言を聞いて驚き恐み、太刀十口、鉾二枚、鉄弓二張、鉄箭二具、許呂四口、枚鉄一連、練鉄一連、馬一匹、鞍一具、八咫鏡二面、五色の絁一連の幣帛を[[鹿島神宮|神宮]]に奉納したとされる。
『常陸国風土記』には神聞勝命と[[倭武天皇]]に使える[[臣狭山命]]が登場することから、中臣氏本宗家である神聞勝命、[[国摩大鹿島命]]、臣狭山命の三代ほどが東国で活動した可能性がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、32-37頁。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[梨津臣命]]、母は額田部連の祖・坂戸毘古命の娘である冨炊屋媛命で、[[伊豆国造]]の祖・波刈禰命の娘である美加々比売命を娶って、[[久志宇賀主命]]と[[荒木田氏]]の祖・伊己呂比命を生んだとされる<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「青木」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref>。
久志宇賀主命は[[国摩大鹿島命]]と同じ[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]の人物とされ、これに従えば大鹿島命とは同時代の人物となる。また久志宇賀主命の別名を探湯主命ともいい、この[[探湯]]の名は託宣の名を持つ神聞勝命にも通じる。これらの理由から久志宇賀主命が実際には神聞勝命か大鹿島命のどちらかと同一人物と考えられる。その場合、大鹿島命の実際の父は神聞勝命、母は伊豆国造の娘である美加々比売命と見られる。しかし神聞勝命と久志宇賀主命は早くに別人化したものと見られ、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では同人説と別人説の二種類の世系が記載されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、28-35頁。</ref>。
また[[鈴木真年]]の著した『華族諸家伝』や『日本事物原始』によると、崇神朝に兄勝命と弟勝命が玉祖氏の祖・小塩命に同行して東国に到来し、[[多摩郡]]で[[阿伎留神社|阿伎留大神]]と大麻止乃豆神を祀り、前者の後裔が[[武蔵国]]と[[伊豆国]]の[[卜部氏]]に、後者の後裔が[[常陸国]]の卜部氏や殖栗連となったとされる。この兄弟はどの系図でも神聞勝命とは別人とされるが、殖栗連は国摩大鹿島命の子・大楯命の後裔とされており、時期や居住地を考えても弟勝命と同一人物と見られる<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、38頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[占部]] *[[卜部氏]]

久志宇賀主命は[[国摩大鹿島命]]と同じ[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]の人物とされ、これに従えば大鹿島命とは同時代の人物となる。また久志宇賀主命の別名を探湯主命ともいい、この[[探湯]]の名は託宣の名を持つ神聞勝命にも通じる。これらの理由から久志宇賀主命が実際には神聞勝命か大鹿島命のどちらかと同一人物と考えられる。その場合、大鹿島命の実際の父は神聞勝命、母は伊豆国造の娘である美加々比売命と見られる。しかし神聞勝命と久志宇賀主命は早くに別人化したものと見られ、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では同人説と別人説の二種類の世系が記載されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、28-35頁。</ref>。
また[[鈴木真年]]の著した『華族諸家伝』や『日本事物原始』によると、崇神朝に兄勝命と弟勝命が玉祖氏の祖・小塩命に同行して東国に到来し、[[多摩郡]]で[[阿伎留神社|阿伎留大神]]と大麻止乃豆神を祀り、前者の後裔が[[武蔵国]]と[[伊豆国]]の[[卜部氏]]に、後者の後裔が[[常陸国]]の卜部氏や殖栗連となったとされる。この兄弟はどの系図でも神聞勝命とは別人とされるが、殖栗連は国摩大鹿島命の子・大楯命の後裔とされており、時期や居住地を考えても弟勝命と同一人物と見られる<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、38頁。</ref>。

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨迹臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = [[建稲穂命]]、[[建御世狭名命]]、[[兄勝命]]、[[弟勝命]]？ | 妻 = [[美加々比売命]] | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]]、[[伊己呂比命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 『[[常陸国風土記]]』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に登場する人物で、同書によると[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に神が[[二上山|大坂山]]頂に白妙の大御服を着て顕現し、白鉾の御杖を取って神勅を下したとされる。そこで天皇は八十の伴緒を召集し、神託を下した神について問うと、大中臣神聞勝命が「大八嶋国は汝が知ろすべき国と言向け賜った、香嶋国に坐す[[タケミカヅチ|天津大御神]]（鹿島神）の下された教戒です」と答えた。天皇はこの言を聞いて驚き恐み、太刀十口、鉾二枚、鉄弓二張、鉄箭二具、許呂四口、枚鉄一連、練鉄一連、馬一匹、鞍一具、八咫鏡二面、五色の絁一連の幣帛を[[鹿島神宮|神宮]]に奉納したとされる。

== 系譜 == 父は[[梨津臣命]]、母は[[額田部氏|額田部連]]の祖・[[坂戸毘古命]]の娘である冨炊屋媛命で、[[伊豆国造]]の祖・波刈禰命の娘である美加々比売命を娶って、[[久志宇賀主命]]と[[荒木田氏]]の祖・伊己呂比命を生んだとされる<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「青木」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref>。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨迹臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = [[建稲穂命]]？、[[建御世狭名命]]？、[[兄勝命]]？、[[弟勝命]]？ | 妻 = [[美加々比売命]]？ | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]]？、[[伊己呂比命]]？ | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 『[[常陸国風土記]]』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に登場する人物で、同書によると[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に神が[[二上山|大坂山]]頂に白妙の大御服を着て顕現し、白鉾の御杖を取って神勅を下したとされる。そこで天皇は八十の伴緒を召集し、神託を下した神について問うと、大中臣神聞勝命が「大八嶋国は汝が知ろすべき国と言向け賜った、香嶋国に坐す[[タケミカヅチ|天津大御神]]（鹿島神）の下された教戒です」と答えた。天皇はこの言を聞いて驚き恐み、太刀十口、鉾二枚、鉄弓二張、鉄箭二具、許呂四口、枚鉄一連、練鉄一連、馬一匹、鞍一具、八咫鏡二面、五色の絁一連の幣帛を[[鹿島神宮|神宮]]に奉納したとされる。
『常陸国風土記』には神聞勝命と[[倭武天皇]]に使える[[臣狭山命]]が登場することから、中臣氏本宗家である神聞勝命、[[国摩大鹿島命]]、臣狭山命の三代ほどが東国で活動した可能性がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、32-37頁。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[梨津臣命]]、母は冨炊屋媛命とされる<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「青木」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref>。
=== 宝賀寿男による異説 === 宝賀寿男による主張は以下の通りである。 子とされる久志宇賀主命は[[国摩大鹿島命]]と同じ[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]の人物とされ、これに従えば大鹿島命とは同時代の人物となる。また久志宇賀主命の別名を探湯主命ともいい、この[[探湯]]の名は託宣の名を持つ神聞勝命にも通じる。これらの理由から久志宇賀主命が実際には神聞勝命か大鹿島命のどちらかと同一人物と考えられる。その場合、大鹿島命の実際の父は神聞勝命、母は伊豆国造の娘である美加々比売命と見られる。しかし神聞勝命と久志宇賀主命は早くに別人化したものと見られ、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では同人説と別人説の二種類の世系が記載されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、28-35頁。</ref>。
また[[鈴木真年]]の著した『華族諸家伝』や『日本事物原始』によると、崇神朝に兄勝命と弟勝命が玉祖氏の祖・小塩命に同行して東国に到来し、[[多摩郡]]で[[阿伎留神社|阿伎留大神]]と大麻止乃豆神を祀り、前者の後裔が[[武蔵国]]と[[伊豆国]]の[[卜部氏]]に、後者の後裔が[[常陸国]]の卜部氏や殖栗連となったとされる。この兄弟はどの系図でも神聞勝命とは別人とされるが、殖栗連は国摩大鹿島命の子・大楯命の後裔とされており、時期や居住地を考えても弟勝命と同一人物と見られる<ref>宝賀寿男「二 中臣氏の初期段階ー占部と中臣氏の祖先たち」『古代氏族の研究⑤ 中臣氏 卜占を担った古代占部の後裔』青垣出版、2014年、38頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:かみききかつのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨迹臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨迹臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 『[[常陸国風土記]]』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に登場する人物で、同書によると[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に神が[[二上山|大坂山]]頂に白妙の大御服を着て顕現し、白鉾の御杖を取って神勅を下したとされる。そこで天皇は八十の伴緒を召集し、神託を下した神について問うと、大中臣神聞勝命が「大八嶋国は汝が知ろすべき国と言向け賜った、香嶋国に坐す[[タケミカヅチ|天津大御神]]（鹿島神）の下された教戒です」と答えた。天皇はこの言を聞いて驚き恐み、太刀十口、鉾二枚、鉄弓二張、鉄箭二具、許呂四口、枚鉄一連、練鉄一連、馬一匹、鞍一具、八咫鏡二面、五色の絁一連の幣帛を[[鹿島神宮|神宮]]に奉納したとされる。
== 系譜 == 父は[[梨津臣命]]、母は冨炊屋媛命とされる<ref>『[[尊卑分脈]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:かみききかつのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saddles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saddles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saddles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == *[[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] *{{Interlanguage link|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saddles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saddles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saqqles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, saqqles, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. {{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}』</ref>

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.

{{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|12=draft|12=draft}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|12=draft|12=draft}}』</ref>

Text replacement - "|二上山|" to "|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|"
12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:神聞勝命 up to rev 91088294
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神聞勝命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大中臣神聞勝命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[梨迹臣命]]、母：[[冨炊屋媛命]] | 兄弟 = | 子 = [[久志宇賀主命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''神聞勝命'''（かみききかつのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 『[[常陸国風土記]]』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に登場する人物で、同書によると[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に神が[[二上山|大坂山]]頂に白妙の大御服を着て顕現し、白鉾の御杖を取って神勅を下したとされる。そこで天皇は八十の伴緒を召集し、神託を下した神について問うと、大中臣神聞勝命が「大八嶋国は汝が知ろすべき国と言向け賜った、香嶋国に坐す[[タケミカヅチ|天津大御神]]（鹿島神）の下された教戒です」と答えた。天皇はこの言を聞いて驚き恐み、太刀十口、鉾二枚、鉄弓二張、鉄箭二具、許呂四口、枚鉄一連、練鉄一連、馬一匹、鞍一具、八咫鏡二面、五色の絁一連の幣帛を[[鹿島神宮|神宮]]に奉納したとされる。
== 系譜 == 父は[[梨津臣命]]、母は冨炊屋媛命とされる<ref>『[[尊卑分脈]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:かみききかつのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神聞勝命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Kushiukameshi no Mikoto|qid=Q106106958}}|father={{ill|Nashitomi|qid=Q108616765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106106971]] Wikidata name: Kamikikikatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106106971]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōnakatomi Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|qq=|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}}, Mother: {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579611}} | children = {{ill|Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiukanushi no Mikoto|qq=|1=Kushiukameshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106106958}} }}
'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]], the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|qq=|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579611}}.<ref>『[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == *[[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] *{{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]], the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}}.<ref>『[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]』</ref>

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]], the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}}.<ref>『[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]』</ref>

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]], the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}}.<ref>『[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == *[[:en:Radical 25|Radical 25]] *{{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]], the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}}.<ref>『[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{translated page|ja|神聞勝命|version=91088294|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|WD=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}』</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == *{{ill|Radical 25|en|Radical 25|WD=Q94724|lt=Radical 25}} *{{ill|Urabe clan|lt=Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|clan Urabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}

'''Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto''' (神聞勝命) was an ancient Japanese noble and an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Overview == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto is mentioned in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* in the Kashima District section. According to the text, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, a deity appeared at the summit of {{ill|Futakami-yama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami-yama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, wearing white divine robes and holding a white halberd. Upon receiving divine instructions, the emperor gathered eighty officials to inquire about the god who had descended. Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto identified the deity as {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}}, the celestial god enshrined in Kashima Province, declaring that the god's instructions were meant for governing the land of Ōyashima. Upon hearing this, the emperor, astonished and reverent, dedicated offerings to the shrine, including swords, halberds, bows, arrows, iron tools, horses, sa11les, mirrors, and fabrics in five colors.
== Genealogy == Kamikikikatsu no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Nashitomi no Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi no Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108616765}} and {{ill|Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja|冨炊屋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579611|1=Tomikiya-hime no Mikoto}}.<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|WD=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}』</ref>
